parameters.name: xsectgrp
parameters.label: X-Section Groups
parameters.description: You can use a subset of primitives from the Cross-section input by specifying a group here. Accepts patterns, as described in .

parameters.name: railgrp
parameters.label: Rail Groups
parameters.description: You can use a subset of primitives from the Rails input by specifying a group here. Accepts patterns, as described in .

parameters.name: cycle
parameters.label: Cycle Type
parameters.description: Select how the cross=section is applied along the rails.

parameters.name: pairs
parameters.label: Sweep along Pairs of Rails
parameters.description: Sweeps along rail 1 & 2, 3 & 4, 5 & 6 etc. instead of 1 & 2, 2 & 3, 3 & 4 etc.

parameters.name: firstl
parameters.label: Sweep along First and Last Rail
parameters.description: Connects the cross-section between the first and last rails.

parameters.name: stretch
parameters.label: Stretch to Rails
parameters.description: Stretches the cross-section geometry to the rail geometry.

parameters.name: usevtx
parameters.label: Use Vertex
parameters.description: Specifies two vertices of the cross section polygon to be placed on rail1 and rail2 respectively. Very useful, for instance, to keep the first vertex on rail1 and the seventh vertex on rail2.

parameters.name: roll
parameters.label: Roll
parameters.description: Non-cumulative rotation of the cross sections around the backbone. All cross sections get the same rotation.

parameters.name: noflip
parameters.label: Fix Flipping
parameters.description: Option to correct the flipping when no direction vector is used and the two rails happen to cross each other causing the normal to flip upside down.

parameters.name: usedir
parameters.label: Use Direction
parameters.description: Uses the direction vector specified in the X, Y and Z coordinates. Otherwise it will use the normals of the geometry.

parameters.name: newg
parameters.label: Create Output Groups
parameters.description: Selecting this option enables the creation of groups. A group is created for each backbone that is incoming. This allows for easy skinning in the .

parameters.name: railname
parameters.label: Group Name
parameters.description: Specify the name of your output groups in this field. Accepts patterns, as described in .

parameters.name: vertex2
parameters.label: Vertex2
parameters.description: The vertices at which the cross-section is connected to the rails.
